title: investigation.yaml (v1/v2)
description: A named collection of Studies grouped under a shared research question, with an explicit cross-study dependency DAG and top-level acceptance criteria. Accepts schema_version 1 (minimal: name/title/question/hypothesis/description/studies/acceptance_criteria) and 2 (current: adds the narrative-spine fields the dnaa-replication investigation evolved — executive, scientific_argument, biological_story, lead, at_a_glance, how_to_read, glossary, guidelines). All v2 fields are optional; a v1 spec validates unchanged. New scaffolds should write schema_version: 2 with the narrative-spine fields present (empty or commented) so they prompt the author to fill them in.

schema_version.description: 1 = legacy v1 (bare name/title/question/hypothesis/description/studies/acceptance_criteria). 2 = current (narrative-spine fields populated). New scaffolds emit 2.

name.description: URL-safe slug that matches the directory name under investigations/.

status.description: Overall status of the investigation. 'planning' is accepted as an alias for 'planned' for back-compat. 'closed' is the terminal post-merge state set by the close-investigation workflow (`/pbg-investigation close <slug>`) after the report.html has been checked in and the PR opened.

question.description: The overarching research question answered by running all member studies in concert.

hypothesis.description: Predicted outcome across the full study sequence.

description.description: Markdown narrative: background, mechanism, study sequence, expected outcome. Superseded for new specs by the v2 `biological_story` (multi-paragraph mechanism prose) + `lead` (3-4 sentence intro) fields, which separate the front-of-textbook summary from the in-depth mechanism narrative. Kept for v1 back-compat.

studies.description: Ordered list of study slugs that belong to this investigation. Controls dashboard grouping/visibility. The DAG topology is derived from each study's own parent_studies field.

expert_docs.description: Names of expert_docs entries from workspace.yaml that are relevant to this investigation (cross-reference convenience, not authoritative).

acceptance_criteria.description: Top-level success criteria for the investigation. Each entry maps to a specific expected_behavior[i].name on a member study. The investigation is 'complete' when all entries pass.

study.description: Slug of the member study that owns this criterion.

behavior.description: The expected_behavior[i].name value on the named study.

executive.description: v2 narrative field — executive summary panel rendered at the top of the report. Lets a reviewer who won't read the studies see what the investigation concluded, where it stands, and what the open decisions are. dnaa-replication example: {what_is_this, verdict, verdict_status, decisions_needed: ['expert opinion on RIDA vs DDAH', ...]}.

what_is_this.description: One-paragraph 'what an investigation is, and what THIS investigation is about'. Reader-friendly.

verdict.description: Headline summary of where the investigation currently stands.

verdict_status.description: Common values: in-progress | passing | passing-with-caveats | failing | inconclusive | not-yet-run.

decisions_needed.description: Open decisions blocking forward progress. Each entry is a short string or {question, owner, context} object.

scientific_argument.description: v2 narrative field — the structured claim/evidence panel. Separates the main claim from the evidence that supports/contradicts it, the key figures, and known caveats. Distinct from `executive` (which is the bottom-line verdict) — this is the chain of reasoning.

main_claim.description: The investigation's primary claim, as a single statement.

evidence_for.description: Bullets supporting the main claim. Free-form.

evidence_against.description: Bullets contradicting the main claim. Free-form.

key_figures.description: Pointers to viz/report assets supporting the argument (paths or anchor names).

caveats.description: Known limitations of the argument as currently posed.

interpretation_ref.description: Path to a longer interpretation document, when one exists.

biological_story.description: v2 narrative field — multi-paragraph plain-English mechanism narrative. The 'textbook chapter' summary of what the investigation is modeling. Markdown allowed. Distinct from `description` (legacy free-form) and `lead` (the 3-4 sentence intro).

lead.description: v2 narrative field — 3-4 sentence front-of-textbook intro. The first thing a reader sees. Lands above biological_story in the rendered report.

at_a_glance.description: v2 narrative field — one-line role per study. Lets a reviewer see what each study CONTRIBUTES to the overall arc without opening any of them. Each entry: {study: <slug>, role: '<one-line role>'}.

role.description: One sentence: what this study contributes to the arc.

how_to_read.description: v2 narrative field — evaluator tips. 'Read studies in order. Each study's `report.verdict` block is the headline. Open the rendered viz only when a primary test fails.' Markdown allowed.

glossary.description: v2 narrative field — investigation-local term definitions. Lets a reader who doesn't know the domain look up DnaA-ATP, RIDA, DDAH inline. Each entry: {term, definition}.

guidelines.description: v2 narrative field — investigation-wide rules: literature anchors, parameter tables, naming conventions, calibration thresholds that every member study should respect. Free-form sub-fields; common ones include `literature_anchors[]`, `parameter_catalog[]`, `naming_conventions`, `calibration_targets`.

contributors.description: S5: who built this investigation. Auto-derived by `/pbg-investigation close` from `git log --pretty='%an|%ae' <main>..<branch>` plus any agent-session metadata under .pbg/agent-sessions/, then editable by the author. Survives squash-merges where git authors would otherwise be lost. Lets future contributors see who came before and gives credit when others build on top.

kind.description: human = git commit author; agent = recorded via .pbg/agent-sessions/.

roles.description: Roles played on this investigation. Free-form; common values: designer | implementer | reviewer | agent_runner | data_curator. A single contributor may have multiple roles.

commits.description: Count of commits attributed to this contributor on the investigation branch (since divergence from main). Populated by the close mechanic; informational only.

sessions.description: For agents: list of session ids (UUIDs from .pbg/agent-sessions/<id>.json) attributed to this contributor.

notes.description: Optional free-form acknowledgement (e.g., 'expert review of dnaa-02 implementation').

closed_at.description: S5: ISO-8601 timestamp when `/pbg-investigation close` stamped the investigation. Set automatically by the close mechanic; not user-edited. Distinct from `created` (start of work).

report_url.description: S5: path to the checked-in HTML report. Relative to the investigation directory (typically just 'report.html'). Set automatically by the close mechanic when it copies the rendered report into investigations/<slug>/report.html. Lets the dashboard link to the immutable snapshot of what the investigation produced.
